name: Study Identifier
definition: Identifier for the overall study or project the sample belongs to.
curation_note: 'default-study' if not part of a special study, otherwise the structured form. Treat as a human label, not the primary key.

name: Study Tags
definition: Keywords describing the study's focus or characteristics.
curation_note: May be left blank.

name: Collector Name
examples: Angela Fernando; Jane Doe; John Smith
definition: Full name(s) of the individual(s) who collected the specimen.
curation_note: Present, spell-checked, consistent formatting. Record multiple collectors as separate values.

name: Collector Organization
examples: Prenni Lab, Colorado State University; Mahidol University, INMU
definition: Affiliated organisation or institution of the collector(s).
curation_note: Present and correctly spelled.

name: Specimen ID
definition: Persistent, globally unique identifier minted when a specimen comes into being at collection. Should resolve via a PTFI base URL.
curation_note: Assigned by the curator; the resolvable form anchors all downstream samples and aliquots.

name: Collection Date
definition: Date the food was collected — harvest date for an agricultural product, or purchase date for food sourced at market.
curation_note: Present and ISO 8601 formatted (YYYY-MM-DD).

name: Collection Latitude
definition: Latitude of the collection site in decimal degrees, WGS84 datum.
curation_note: Numeric, in range. If absent, query the collector. Round deliberately where the precise site is sensitive.

name: Collection Longitude
definition: Longitude of the collection site in decimal degrees, WGS84 datum.
curation_note: Numeric, in range. If absent, query the collector.

name: Collection Country
definition: Country where the food was collected, recorded once as an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code. The display name is derived from the code.
curation_note: Mandatory. Store the code canonically; do not also store the country name as a separate free-text field.

name: Collection Administrative Area
definition: Subnational administrative area of collection (state, region, province, or district). Use an ISO 3166-2 subdivision code where one exists; otherwise the named area.
curation_note: Add when known. Prefer the standard code so the level is comparable across countries.

name: Collection Locality
examples: Damnoen Saduak Floating Market; Fort Collins
definition: Most specific named place of collection — city, town, market, farm, or named site. A gazetteer identifier (GeoNames or Wikidata) is recommended for interoperability.
curation_note: Add when available; capture a gazetteer ID alongside free text where possible.

name: Production Type
allowed_values: conventional; organic; grain-fed; grass-fed; open field; hydroponic/aquaponic; greenhouse; farmed; wild; unknown; other
definition: Production system in which the specimen was produced.
curation_note: One or more values, lowercase.

name: Specimen Unit Type
allowed_values: individual; group of individuals; liquid; unknown
definition: Form in which the specimen was collected.
curation_note: individual (e.g. 1 apple); group of individuals (e.g. a bag of rice); liquid (e.g. oil). Must be consistent with size and unit.

name: Specimen Unit Size
definition: Number of individuals or amount of specimen collected.
curation_note: Positive value consistent with the unit (e.g. 3 apples; 150 g rice; 0.50 L oil). Leave blank if unknown.

name: Specimen Unit Size Unit
definition: Unit in which the specimen size is expressed, from the Unit Ontology — including count for individuals.
curation_note: Use a UO term. 'count' covers individual items.

name: Specimen Temperature at Collection
definition: Temperature condition under which the specimen was stored at collection (e.g. flash-frozen in liquid nitrogen = controlled).
curation_note: Select from the temperature vocabulary. Consider recording a cold-chain category or value where known, since thermal history drives composition.

name: Procurement Type
allowed_values: field; fresh market; small grocery; supermarket; biobank; unknown; other
definition: The kind of place where the specimen was procured.
curation_note: Select one value from the procurement vocabulary.

name: Specimen Origin Location
definition: Country of origin of the specimen, which may differ from where it was collected (e.g. collected in a US supermarket, originating from MX).
curation_note: Present and logical; an ISO 3166-1 code in every row.

name: Specimen Food Product Name
definition: Standardised food-product name for the specimen, aligned with FoodOn. Include the cultivar where known.
curation_note: Lowercase, typo-free. If a cultivar is known, include it (e.g. 'gala apple', not 'apple').

name: Specimen Food Product IRI
definition: FoodOn IRI for the specimen's food-product form — a subclass of 'food product by organism', as specific as possible (cultivar where present).
curation_note: Match name + preparation + method; avoid 'efsa foodex2' terms. If none exists, file a term request (SOP 3).

name: Specimen Common Name
definition: Vernacular name of the specimen in the collector's local language or dialect. Pair with the language tag.
curation_note: Exact spelling/spacing. Confirm it denotes a meaningful food (e.g. 'hoy dong' = fermented shellfish), not just 'fish'.

name: Common Name Language Code
definition: Language of the common name as a BCP 47 tag (language, optional script, optional region).
curation_note: One tag per common-name value. BCP 47 covers three-letter (ISO 639-3) languages and scripts too.

name: Specimen Organism Name
format: Latin binomial — Genus capitalised, species lowercase
examples: Zea mays var. saccharata; Malus domestica
definition: Scientific (Latin) name of the source organism. Use genus when several species apply.
curation_note: Roman form, correct case and spelling. For multi-ingredient foods record the principal organism (or leave for curation) rather than omitting it.

name: Specimen Organism IRI
definition: Most specific NCBI Taxonomy IRI for the source organism, retrieved via OLS.
curation_note: Search the organism name in OLS; record the IRI of the best, most specific match.

name: Specimen Breed or Cultivar
definition: Cultivar, variety, or hybrid (plants) or breed (animals); or 'unknown'.
curation_note: Record when known, lowercase, spelling consistent with accepted names.

name: Specimen Preparation
definition: Preparation state of the specimen at collection.
curation_note: One or more values from the preparation vocabulary.

name: Specimen Preparation Method
allowed_values: boiled; fried; steamed; grilled; en papillote; fermented; smoked; dried; canned; ground; low or reduced fat; pasteurized; fresh frozen; hulled; dehydrated; enriched or fortified; pressure cooked; roasted; sautéed; other; unknown
definition: Method(s) applied when the specimen is cooked or processed.
curation_note: Encode all applicable methods. Required only when preparation is cooked or processed.

name: Specimen ABS Status
definition: Access and Benefit-Sharing status under the Nagoya Protocol and national genetic-resource law, as an ordinal level.
curation_note: Ordinal from the ABS vocabulary. Where the level indicates clearance is needed, publication is blocked until ethical/legal sign-off.

name: Sample ID
definition: Persistent, globally unique identifier for the sample. Should resolve via a PTFI base URL.
curation_note: Assigned by the curator; links the analysed material to its specimen.

name: Study Sample Identifier
definition: Human-facing identifier for the sample within a study.
curation_note: Valid, unique, no stray spaces.

name: Sample Food Product Name
definition: Food-product name as recorded by the laboratory; may differ from the specimen when the sample is a specific part or process (e.g. beet greens vs. beet root).
curation_note: Present, lowercase, singular, spelling verified.

name: Sample Common Name
definition: Vernacular name(s) for the sample as used locally. Pair each with a language tag.
curation_note: Optional; tag each name with its BCP 47 language.

name: Sample Food Product IRI
definition: FoodOn IRI for the sample's food-product form; may equal the specimen IRI when preparation and processing are identical.
curation_note: Same procedure as the specimen IRI, using the sample's name, preparation, and method.

name: Sample Lab Location
definition: Laboratory responsible for preparing or analysing the sample.
curation_note: Present and correctly spelled; if missing, query the author.

name: Sample Preparer Name
definition: Name of the person who prepared the sample in the lab.
curation_note: Present and correctly spelled.

name: Sample Preparation Date
definition: Date on which the sample was prepared.
curation_note: ISO 8601 format. If unknown, leave null and flag the row — never write a placeholder string into the date field.

name: Sample Preparation
allowed_values: same as specimen; cooked; processed; unknown
definition: Preparation state of the sample.
curation_note: If unchanged from the specimen, select 'same as specimen'. Occurrence is multiple.

name: Sample Preparation Method
allowed_values: boiled; fried; steamed; grilled; en papillote; fermented; smoked; dried; canned; ground; low or reduced fat; pasteurized; fresh frozen; hulled; dehydrated; enriched or fortified; pressure cooked; roasted; sautéed; other; unknown
definition: Method(s) used to prepare the sample when cooked or processed (shares the preparation-method vocabulary).
curation_note: Encode all applicable methods. Required when preparation is cooked or processed.

name: Sample Laboratory Processing
definition: How the sample was processed in the lab prior to freezer storage.
curation_note: Select from the lab-processing vocabulary.

name: Sample Wet Weight
definition: Wet (fresh) weight of the sample in grams, after inedible parts are removed.
curation_note: Required; positive. If blank, query the author.

name: Sample Dry Weight
definition: Weight of the sample after drying, in grams.
curation_note: Present where applicable; positive value.

name: Curation Status
definition: Controlled stage of the record's metadata curation.
curation_note: 'required' before curation; 'complete' once all fields are verified.

name: Aliquot ID
definition: Persistent, globally unique identifier for the analytical aliquot, linking measurements back to sample and specimen.
curation_note: Assigned by the curator.

name: Aliquot Weight
definition: Weight of the individual analytical aliquot in milligrams.
curation_note: Numeric, positive, where an aliquot was drawn for analysis.
